name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 63 Thursday. SMH $499.58 (+1.7% 1D, +37.8% 1M, +23.8% 3M) — RECOVERING APR 28 -3.0% SELLOFF ON MAG 7 BEAT-AND-RAISE CAPEX; AI-CAPEX THESIS STRUCTURALLY VINDICATED. WSJ Tue Apr 28 reported OpenAI MISSED Q1 INTERNAL TARGETS (1B WAU goal, actual ~900M); CFO Sarah Friar told leadership she is worried about ability to fund future compute contracts if revenue doesn't accelerate. AI-spend-trajectory thesis cracks: if OpenAI (demand anchor for Nvidia/AMD/Broadcom hyperscaler-scale orders) slows, the entire AI-capex stack reprices. Tuesday cascade: Nvidia -1.5% (after Mon record high), AMD -3.1%, Broadcom -3.29%, Arm -6.7%, Intel/Micron/Applied Materials all down. Wed: SMH ETF -3.0% (biggest down-day since Mar 30 per StockAnalysis); URA -4.4% (AI-data-center-demand thesis runs through SMR uranium contracts; AI doubt = uranium doubt); KOSPI +0.8% DECOUPLED (Korean memory/HBM has its own cycle — SK Hynix/Samsung beneficiaries); TAIEX -0.6% modest pullback. CRITICAL INVESTOR READ: 'tech = uncorrelated to West Asia' mental model is correct here — AI selloff is endogenous to AI's own funding/adoption cycle. Crisis resolving does NOT fix OpenAI's revenue miss. Conversely, war ending while AI-cycle deteriorates means tech keeps falling even if Brent crashes. MAG 7 EARNINGS TONIGHT (after Wed close): Microsoft Q3 (Azure growth, OpenAI deal revenue cap), Meta (capex commitments $115-135B), Alphabet ($175-185B capex), Amazon ($200B capex). These four are the NEXT-24-HOUR BINARY DETERMINANT for AI-capex narrative. Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured Apr 27 adds governance uncertainty to demand uncertainty. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
